steps.description: Use the sine-cosine form of cotangent
steps.explanation: Writing cotangent as cosine over sine is convenient here because both exact values are simple to substitute.

steps.description: Recall the exact value of cosine 30 degrees
steps.explanation: This is a standard exact value from the $30$-$60$-$90$ triangle.

steps.description: Recall the exact value of sine 30 degrees
steps.explanation: This is the matching exact value from the same special triangle.
Placing cosine over sine gives a fraction divided by a fraction, ready to simplify.
Dividing by $\frac{1}{2}$ is the same as multiplying by $2$, and the twos cancel to leave $\sqrt{3}$.
This is the exact value; note it is already in simplest surd form with no denominator to rationalise.
